T1046.name: Network Service Scanning
T1046.description: Adversaries scan networks to map systems and discover open ports and services.

T1021.001.name: Remote Services: Remote Desktop Protocol
T1021.001.description: Adversaries log in interactively to remote systems using RDP to execute programs.

T1021.002.name: Remote Services: SMB/Windows Admin Shares
T1021.002.description: Adversaries use Windows SMB shares to write files, run programs, and move laterally.

T1021.004.name: Remote Services: SSH
T1021.004.description: Adversaries connect to remote terminals using SSH to operate target servers.

T1021.005.name: Remote Services: VNC
T1021.005.description: Adversaries use VNC graphical sharing systems to hijack mouse and keyboard control.

T1021.006.name: Remote Services: Windows Remote Management
T1021.006.description: Adversaries use WinRM (PowerShell Remoting) to run remote scripts on targets.

T1110.name: Brute Force
T1110.description: Adversaries run dictionary attacks to guess passwords and access systems.

T1110.003.name: Password Spraying
T1110.003.description: Adversaries test a single common password against many accounts to bypass locking rules.

T1190.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
T1190.description: Adversaries exploit bugs in public web applications or protocols to execute code.

T1558.003.name: Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets: Kerberoasting
T1558.003.description: Adversaries request Service Principal Name tickets to crack account passwords offline.

T1558.004.name: Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets: AS-REP Roasting
T1558.004.description: Adversaries request tickets for accounts with pre-auth disabled to crack passwords offline.

T1040.name: Network Sniffing
T1040.description: Adversaries sniff traffic (like Telnet or FTP sessions) to capture cleartext credentials.

T1590.005.name: Search Technical Databases: DNS/Domain Records
T1590.005.description: Adversaries query DNS zone transfers (AXFR) to map active subdomains and host layouts.

T1087.002.name: Account Discovery: Domain Account
T1087.002.description: Adversaries query LDAP directory databases to list Active Directory user profiles.

T1069.002.name: Permission Groups Discovery: Domain Groups
T1069.002.description: Adversaries query LDAP groups to identify domain administrators and target groups.

T1083.name: File and Directory Discovery
T1083.description: Adversaries explore files and directory paths to look for configurations and database backups.

T1059.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
T1059.description: Adversaries use command-line interpreter shells (cmd, bash) to run host commands.

T1059.001.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell
T1059.001.description: Adversaries execute PowerShell scripts in-memory to execute post-exploitation tools.

T1078.name: Valid Accounts
T1078.description: Adversaries log in using stolen, brute-forced, or default credentials.

T1043.name: Commonly Used Port
T1043.description: Adversaries use standard ports (like 443) to hide outbound C2 communication traffic.

T1505.003.name: Server Software Component: Web Shell
T1505.003.description: Adversaries upload script files inside web folders to maintain persistent terminal access.

T1598.name: Phishing for Information
T1598.description: Adversaries route mail relays to send phishing messages and harvest credentials.

T1586.name: Compromise Accounts
T1586.description: Adversaries exploit unhardened relays to capture or spoof valid user accounts.
